items.q: What is the capacity of short-term memory according to Miller?
items.why: Students confuse capacity (items) with duration (about 18-30 seconds without rehearsal). Miller's magic number describes how many CHUNKS, not how long.

items.q: What are the three elements of Beck's negative triad?
items.correct: Negative views of self, world, and future.
items.misconception: Negative views of past, present, and future.
items.why: Students often substitute 'past' for 'self' or 'world'. The triad is about the DOMAINS of negative thought (self/world/future), not time periods.

items.correct: Learning by observing the consequences others receive for behaviour.
items.misconception: Being directly rewarded for copying someone.
items.why: Students confuse vicarious with direct reinforcement. 'Vicarious' means experienced through someone else - you see them rewarded/punished and adjust your own behaviour accordingly.
Correct answer: Bobo doll. “Bell” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Learning from others' results. “Learning by yourself only” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Bandura's study year was 1961. Years ago from 2026?
items.why: The real figure is 65. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
